name: crm_short_circuit_not_applicable
description:
Pins the third and final branch of the CRM rule-1.5 short-circuit at engine/assessor.py:733-746 with responsibility='not_applicable' — the last value in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET = frozenset({provider, inherited, not_applicable}) at assessor.py:468. Completes the trio with crm_short_circuit_inherited (→ Compliant) and crm_short_circuit_provider (→ NA via provider).

responsibility='not_applicable' is structurally symmetric to provider in _finalize_crm_decision's status_map at assessor.py:1858-1862 — both map to status=NOT_APPLICABLE + narrative_class=NA_JUSTIFYING. The semantic distinction is intent: 'provider' means "someone else implements this control," while 'not_applicable' means "this control does not apply to this system at all" (e.g., AC-19 mobile device controls on a system that does not permit mobile devices to connect). Both are legitimate CRM authoring patterns; the kernel treats both identically once the short-circuit fires.

This case also pins the f-string at assessor.py:2019 (f'crm_{cloud_r or "na"}') on the literal value 'not_applicable' — proving the source composition stays verbatim from the CRM responsibility token rather than being normalized or remapped. source='crm_not_applicable' is therefore the assertion (NOT 'crm_na' — the 'na' fallback only fires when cloud_r is None/falsy, which never happens for an explicit CRM entry).

llm_stub_proposals=[] + llm_calls=0 is load-bearing — empty queue ensures a kernel regression that bypassed the gate would IndexError loudly from StubLlmClient.propose_twice rather than silently consult the model. tagged_evidence is intentionally non-empty (a generic system inventory) to prove the short-circuit fires regardless of local artifacts — 'not_applicable' means it doesn't apply, full stop, even if the customer happens to have tangentially-related documentation.

Control ID 'ac-19' chosen as the canonical NA example (Access Control for Mobile Devices on systems that prohibit mobile device connectivity). source_baseline_id=999 matches the sentinel used by the sibling CRM cases so failure messages stay diff-able across the trio.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization establishes usage restrictions and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine mobile device usage policy; confirm restrictions and implementation guidance are defined for organization-controlled mobile devices.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00077777 System Asset Inventory Rev A — enumerates all in-boundary hardware and software components.

crm_entries.narrative: Mobile device controls do not apply to this system; the authorization boundary is a closed-rack server enclave with no wireless interfaces and no provisioned pathway for mobile devices to connect.

expected.narrative_contains_regex: closed-rack server enclave with no wireless interfaces
